---
title: "alcohol thoughts reframe"
format: html
---

## Alcohol Thoughts Reframe

Risky drinking often starts with automatic thoughts such as "One drink will help," "I can control it this time," or "I already ruined today." These thoughts can feel true in the moment because emotions are high.

A more useful approach is to test the thought and replace it with a balanced response that fits your recovery goals.

Thought patterns shape emotion, motivation, and behavior. In high-stress moments, the mind often produces fast, extreme conclusions that feel true but are incomplete. These thoughts can intensify urges and narrow choices.

Learning to examine and reframe thoughts creates distance between impulse and action. Over time, this supports steadier mood, better decisions, and stronger follow-through on recovery goals.

::: callout
### Instructions
Write your actual thought, not the "right" answer. Then challenge it with evidence.
:::

| Automatic thought | Evidence for | Evidence against | Balanced response | Next action |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| | | | | |
| | | | | |
| | | | | |
| | | | | |
